    L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scan_flow_brackets_matched_ix_axiom

    IndexedScannerPlainScalarValid — Phase 3 Step 6d.1e foundation (staging) #

    Status: staging file. Not imported by L4YAML.lean until the Phase 3 Step 6f cutover commit (Guardrail 1). Imports IndexedWellBehaved (for the indexed predicates PlainScalarsValidIx / FlowAwarePSVIx / FlowBracketsMatchedIx) and L4YAML.Proofs.Scanner.IndexedDispatch (for the existing indexed scanner monotonicity lemmas — emit_tokens_size, advance_tokens, unwindIndentsIx_tokens_size_le, etc.).

    Scope of Step 6d.1e (this file) #

    This file is the indexed analogue of L4YAML/Proofs/Production/ScannerPlainScalarValid.lean — the scanner-side chain that proves the indexed scanner output satisfies FlowAwarePSVIx and FlowBracketsMatchedIx. The eventual goal is two proven theorems:

    theorem scan_flow_aware_psv_ix :
    Scanner.Indexed.scanIx input = .ok tokens → FlowAwarePSVIx tokens
theorem scan_flow_brackets_matched_ix :
    Scanner.Indexed.scanIx input = .ok tokens → FlowBracketsMatchedIx tokens

    These discharge (via the consumers in IndexedWellBehaved.indexed_scanner_*_axiom) the obligation flagged in §5c of IndexedWellBehaved.lean.

    Phase 3 sub-step 6d.1e.1 (foundation, prior commit) #

    Foundation tier — structural / algebraic building blocks landed in 6d.1e.1: PSV propagation primitives (§1), flowNestingIx prefix stability + push lemmas (§2), FlowContextPSVIx propagation primitives (§3), FlowNestingInvIx bridge invariant (§4), the 2 staged axioms with tightened preconditions (§7 — relocated from IndexedWellBehaved.lean §5c).

    Phase 3 sub-step 6d.1e.2 (prior commit) — Emit-step + indent stack #

    §5 generic emit-step preservation:

    • PlainScalarsValidIx_push_non_plain — pushing a non-plain token preserves PSV (array-level helper, indexed twin of legacy PlainScalarsValid_push_non_plain);
    • emit_preserves_tokens_at — non-flow emit preserves token values at low indices;
    • emit_new_token_token — the token added by emit tok at the new position is tok;
    • emit_non_plain_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx, emit_non_flow_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx, emit_non_flow_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx.

    §6 indent-stack preservation — per-action lemmas for each of the five indent-stack scanner ops:

    • unwindIndentsLoopIx / unwindIndentsIx: _preserves_prefix, _preserves_flowLevel, _new_tokens_not_plain, _new_tokens_not_flow, _preserves_FlowNestingInvIx, _preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx, _preserves_FlowContextPSVIx;
    • pushSequenceIndentIx / pushMappingIndentIx: _preserves_prefix, _preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx, _preserves_FlowNestingInvIx, _preserves_FlowContextPSVIx;
    • saveSimpleKeyIx: _preserves_prefix, _flowLevel, _new_tokens_not_plain, _new_tokens_not_flow, _preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx, _preserves_FlowNestingInvIx, _preserves_FlowContextPSVIx.

    Phase 3 sub-step 6d.1e.3 (this commit) — Scalar scanners #

    §7 scalar-scanner preservation — per-action lemmas for the two state-transforming scalar scanners (the other four scalar primitives listed in the Blueprint — scanDoubleQuotedIx / scanSingleQuotedIx / scanBlockScalarIx / scanPlainScalarIx — return Option (String × IxCursor input), not a state transformation, so their PSV reasoning lives in the dispatcher arm of scanNextTokenIx_dispatchContent and is deferred to Step 6d.1e.6):

    • §7a emitAt building blocks (proven, ~120 LOC): emitAt-twins of §5 (the scalar scanners use emitAt rather than emit, since they need to carry the startPos-from-before-advance start position): emitAt_tokens_size, emitAt_preserves_tokens_at, emitAt_new_token_token, emitAt_non_plain_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx, emitAt_non_flow_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx, emitAt_non_flow_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx.
    • §7b scanAnchorOrAliasIx preservation *(8 lemmas, 6 axioms
      • 2 proven theorems)*: see Reflection 70 for the record-update-opacity wall hit by direct proof attempts. The 6 axioms (_adds_one_token, _preserves_prefix, _preserves_flowLevel, _new_token_not_plain, _new_token_not_flow, _preserves_FlowNestingInvIx) all carry real (h_ok : scanAnchorOrAliasIx s isAnchor = .ok s') preconditions. The 2 proven theorems (_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx, _preserves_FlowContextPSVIx) compose the 6 staged-as-axiom primitives with §1/§3 prefix-and-new combinators.
    • §7c scanTagIx preservation (8 lemmas, same 6+2 split as §7b): identical-shape suite, with three-way case split on the verbatim/secondary/named tag branches.

    Phase 3 closure axiom count after Step 6d.1e.3: 14 axioms — 2 pre-existing (§8 top-level) + 12 new (6 each in §7b/§7c for the state-transforming scalar scanners). The PSV/FlowContextPSVIx preservation theorems (4 total: 2 per scanner) are proven, taking the per-scanner adds_one_token / preserves_prefix / new_token_not_plain axioms as inputs and composing them with the §1 and §3 propagation primitives.

    Phase 3 sub-steps 6d.1e.4+ (future commits) #

    Remaining per-action preservation chain: block-context dispatchers (scanBlockEntryIx, scanKeyIx, scanValueIx, etc.), flow-context dispatchers (flow seq/map start/end, flow entry), document/directive layers + top-level dispatch composition. The dispatcher-level proof in 6d.1e.6 will stage scanPlainScalarIx_content_valid as a new axiom (or discharge it inline, depending on the Layer F.4 integration cost). Final 6d.1e.7 discharges all axioms (the 2 §8 + the 12 §7 + any added in 6d.1e.4–6d.1e.6) with proven theorems built from this chain. See Blueprint Reflections 68 + 70.

    What this file does NOT contain (yet) #

    • Dispatcher / document-directive scanner preservation lemmas (deferred to 6d.1e.4+).
    • Direct proofs of the §7b/§7c scalar-scanner preservation primitives (staged as axioms — see Reflection 70).
    • The two §8 top-level theorems' proofs (deferred to 6d.1e.7).

    Reflection 67 follow-up (Reflection 68) #

    The original ~700 LOC / 1-session estimate for §5c axiom discharge was based on counting the API-surface theorems (scan_flow_aware_psv + scan_flow_brackets_matched = 2 names) and multiplying by an assumed-small dependency-chain factor. The actual chain is ~80 per-action preservation lemmas spread across the 5,584 LOC of legacy ScannerPlainScalarValid.lean plus supporting dependencies (ScannerCorrectness.lean at 10,637 LOC, etc.). The rescoping into 6d.1e.1 (foundation, this commit) + 6d.1e.2+ (chain, ~5 future sessions) lets each commit stay lake build green per Guardrail 1. See Blueprint Reflection 68.

    §1 PSV propagation primitives #

    source
    📐 coverage

    WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx (Indexed.TokenStream.empty input)

    An empty token stream is trivially PlainScalarsValidIx. Indexed twin of legacy PlainScalarsValid_empty.

    source
    📐 coverage
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.PlainScalarsValidIx_of_prefix_and_new {input : String} (old_tokens new_tokens : Indexed.TokenStream input) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx old_tokens) (h_mono : old_tokens.size ≤ new_tokens.size) (h_prefix : ∀ (i : Nat) (hi : i < old_tokens.size), new_tokens[i] = old_tokens[i]) (h_new : ∀ (j : Nat) (hj : j < new_tokens.size), j ≥ old_tokens.size → match new_tokens[j].token with | YamlToken.scalar content ScalarStyle.plain => Grammar.ScalarScannable { content := content, style := ScalarStyle.plain } false | x => True) :
    WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx new_tokens

    Prefix preservation + new-tokens PSV ⟹ PSV for extended stream. Indexed twin of legacy PlainScalarsValid_of_prefix_and_new.

      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.psv_match_of_ne_plain_ix {input : String} (tokens : Indexed.TokenStream input) (j : Nat) (hj : j < tokens.size) (h_ne : ∀ (c : String), tokens[j].token ≠ YamlToken.scalar c ScalarStyle.plain) :
      match tokens[j].token with | YamlToken.scalar content ScalarStyle.plain => Grammar.ScalarScannable { content := content, style := ScalarStyle.plain } false | x => True

      When a token is provably not .scalar _ .plain, the PSV match is True. Indexed twin of legacy psv_match_of_ne_plain.

      match tok.token with | YamlToken.scalar content ScalarStyle.plain => Grammar.ScalarScannable { content := content, style := ScalarStyle.plain } false | x => True

      §2 flowNestingIx prefix stability and push lemmas #

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_go_prefix_stable {input : String} (old new : Array (Indexed.IxToken input)) (h_mono : old.size ≤ new.size) (h_prefix_val : ∀ (j : Nat) (hj : j < old.size), new[j].token = old[j].token) (pos target depth : Nat) (h_target : target ≤ old.size) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go new pos target depth = WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go old pos target depth

      flowNestingIx.go is stable under prefix-preserving array extension. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_go_prefix_stable.

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_prefix_stable {input : String} (old new : Indexed.TokenStream input) (h_mono : old.size ≤ new.size) (h_prefix_val : ∀ (j : Nat) (hj : j < old.size), new[j].token = old[j].token) (i : Nat) (hi : i ≤ old.size) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx new i = WellBehaved.flowNestingIx old i

      flowNestingIx at positions ≤ old.size is unchanged by stream extension. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_prefix_stable.

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_go_single_push {input : String} (tokens : Array (Indexed.IxToken input)) (t : Indexed.IxToken input) (depth : Nat) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go (tokens.push t) tokens.size (tokens.size + 1) depth = match t.token with | YamlToken.flowSequenceStart => depth + 1 | YamlToken.flowMappingStart => depth + 1 | YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd => if depth > 0 then depth - 1 else 0 | YamlToken.flowMappingEnd => if depth > 0 then depth - 1 else 0 | x => depth

      Processing a single pushed token at the end of the array. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_go_single_push.

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_push {input : String} (tokens : Array (Indexed.IxToken input)) (t : Indexed.IxToken input) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go (tokens.push t) 0 (tokens.size + 1) 0 = match t.token with | YamlToken.flowSequenceStart => WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0 + 1 | YamlToken.flowMappingStart => WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0 + 1 | YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd => if WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0 > 0 then WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0 - 1 else 0 | YamlToken.flowMappingEnd => if WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0 > 0 then WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0 - 1 else 0 | x => WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0

      How flowNestingIx on the underlying array changes when a single token is appended. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_push.

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_push_non_flow {input : String} (tokens : Array (Indexed.IxToken input)) (t : Indexed.IxToken input) (h1 : t.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart) (h2 : t.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart) (h3 : t.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd) (h4 : t.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go (tokens.push t) 0 (tokens.size + 1) 0 = WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens 0 tokens.size 0

      Appending a non-flow token preserves flowNestingIx at the old size. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_push_non_flow.

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_go_non_flow {input : String} (tokens : Array (Indexed.IxToken input)) (pos target depth : Nat) (h_nf : ∀ (j : Nat), pos ≤ j → j < target → ∀ (hj : j < tokens.size), tokens[j].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ tokens[j].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ tokens[j].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ tokens[j].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens pos target depth = depth

      flowNestingIx.go on a range of non-flow tokens returns depth unchanged. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_go_non_flow.

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_go_setIfInBounds_non_flow {input : String} (tokens : Array (Indexed.IxToken input)) (idx : Nat) (val : Indexed.IxToken input) (h_val_nf : val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) (h_orig_nf : ∀ (h : idx < tokens.size), tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) (pos target depth : Nat) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go (tokens.setIfInBounds idx val) pos target depth = WellBehaved.flowNestingIx.go tokens pos target depth

      Replacing a non-flow slot with another non-flow token preserves flowNestingIx.go. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_go_setIfInBounds_non_flow.

      source
      📐 coverage
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.flowNestingIx_setIfInBounds_non_flow {input : String} (tokens : Indexed.TokenStream input) (idx : Nat) (val : Indexed.IxToken input) (h_val_nf : val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) (h_orig_nf : ∀ (h : idx < tokens.size), tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) (target : Nat) :
      WellBehaved.flowNestingIx (tokens.setIfInBounds idx val) target = WellBehaved.flowNestingIx tokens target

      Replacing a non-flow token at idx with another non-flow token preserves flowNestingIx. Indexed twin of legacy flowNesting_setIfInBounds_non_flow.

      match tok.token with | YamlToken.scalar content ScalarStyle.plain => Grammar.ScalarScannable { content := content, style := ScalarStyle.plain } true | x => True

      When a token is provably not .scalar _ .plain, the FlowContextPSVIx match is True. Indexed twin of legacy fpsv_of_not_plain.

      §4 FlowNestingInvIx — scanner-state invariant #

      FlowNestingInvIx s says the token-array flow-nesting depth at the end of the array equals the scanner's flowLevel field. This is the bridge invariant between flowNestingIx (token-level computation) and the scanner's flowLevel (running state). Preserved through every non-flow-emitting scanner action.

      source
      🔹 coverage
      def L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) :
      Prop

      The scanner-state invariant bridging flowNestingIx to flowLevel. Indexed twin of legacy FlowNestingInv.

      Equations
      • L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.FlowNestingInvIx s = (L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.WellBehaved.flowNestingIx s.tokens s.tokens.size = s.flowLevel)
      Instances For

        §5 Generic emit-step preservation building blocks #

        Unit lemmas — each says "emitting a token with a specific shape preserves one of the three invariants" — used by the per-action preservation chain (§6 indent-stack ops, and the scalar / dispatcher families in Step 6d.1e.3+).

        Landed in Step 6d.1e.2 alongside the indent-stack consumers below.

        Array-level helpers #

        The legacy PlainScalarsValid_push_non_plain operates on the array directly; its indexed analogue threads through TokenStream.push. Used by pushSequenceIndentIx, pushMappingIndentIx to discharge the new-token obligation in one step.

        source
        📐 coverage
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.PlainScalarsValidIx_push_non_plain {input : String} (tokens : Indexed.TokenStream input) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx tokens) (t : Indexed.IxToken input) (h_np : match t.token with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) :
        WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx (tokens.push t)

        Pushing a non-plain token preserves PlainScalarsValidIx. Indexed twin of legacy PlainScalarsValid_push_non_plain.

        State-level emit lemmas #

        emit_preserves_tokens_at lets indent-stack proofs replace (s.emit tok).tokens[i] with s.tokens[i] for in-bounds i.

        source
        📐 coverage
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emit_preserves_tokens_at {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (tok : YamlToken) (i : Nat) (h : i < s.tokens.size) :
        (s.emit tok).tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]

        emit preserves token values at positions below the original size. Indexed twin of legacy emit_preserves_tokens_at.

        source
        📐 coverage
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emit_non_plain_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) (h_np : match tok with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) :
        WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx (s.emit tok).tokens

        Emitting a non-plain token preserves PlainScalarsValidIx. Indexed analogue of the inline pattern used in legacy pushSequenceIndent_preserves_PlainScalarsValid.

        source
        📐 coverage
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emit_non_flow_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) (h1 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart) (h2 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart) (h3 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd) (h4 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
        FlowNestingInvIx (s.emit tok)

        Emitting a non-flow token preserves FlowNestingInvIx. Indexed twin of legacy FlowNestingInv_emit_non_flow.

        source
        📐 coverage
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emit_non_flow_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) (h_np : match tok with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) (_h1 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart) (_h2 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart) (_h3 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd) (_h4 : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
        WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx (s.emit tok).tokens

        Emitting a non-flow, non-plain token preserves FlowContextPSVIx. Composes FlowContextPSVIx_of_prefix_and_new (§3) with flowNestingIx_push_non_flow (§2) and fpsv_of_not_plain_ix on the single new token.

        source
        📐 coverage
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emit_new_token_token {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (tok : YamlToken) (h : s.tokens.size < (s.emit tok).tokens.size) :
        (s.emit tok).tokens[s.tokens.size].token = tok

        The token added by emit tok at the new position is tok. Used by indent-stack new_tokens_not_plain / new_tokens_not_flow proofs to reduce the new-position match to a cases over tok.

        §6 Indent-stack preservation lemmas #

        Per-action preservation for the indent-stack scanner ops (unwindIndentsLoopIx, unwindIndentsIx, pushSequenceIndentIx, pushMappingIndentIx, saveSimpleKeyIx). Each action gets the prefix / flowLevel / new-token / invariant-preservation lemmas the per-dispatcher chain (Step 6d.1e.3+) will consume.

        The legacy counterparts live in:

        • Proofs/Scanner/ScannerCorrectness.lean — prefix / flowLevel / token-count side (lines ~200, ~263, ~280, ~379);
        • Proofs/Production/ScannerPlainScalarValid.lean — invariant preservation (lines ~164, ~1081, ~1091, ~1699, ~1719).

        §6a emit .blockEnd step combinator #

        unwindIndentsLoopIx emits .blockEnd tokens then pops the indent stack. The pop is a record update on indents only and is invisible to all our predicates. This subsection isolates the lemmas about the single .blockEnd emit + pop step.

        source
        🔸 coverage
        @[reducible, inline]
        abbrev L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emitBlockEndPop {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) :
        Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input

        After-emit-and-pop state shape used by unwindIndentsLoopIx.

        Equations
        • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.

          §6f saveSimpleKeyIx preservation #

          saveSimpleKeyIx either leaves tokens unchanged or pushes two .placeholder tokens. Both are non-plain and non-flow, so all the preservation lemmas follow from §5.

          source

          Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input

          Abbreviation for the post-saveSimpleKeyIx state in its two-emit branch (no simpleKey record update — that is invisible to tokens / flowLevel).

          Equations

            saveSimpleKeyIx either leaves tokens unchanged or pushes two placeholder tokens. Eliminates the if-chain in saveSimpleKeyIx so downstream proofs case-split on this disjunction rather than unfolding the body.

            §7 Scalar-scanner per-action preservation (Step 6d.1e.3) #

            State-transforming scalar scanners — scanAnchorOrAliasIx and scanTagIx. Each gets the standard preservation suite: _adds_one_token, _preserves_prefix, _preserves_flowLevel, _new_token_not_plain, _new_token_not_flow, _preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx, _preserves_FlowNestingInvIx, _preserves_FlowContextPSVIx.

            Note on the four pure scalar primitives: scanDoubleQuotedIx, scanSingleQuotedIx, scanBlockScalarIx, and scanPlainScalarIx do not return ScannerStateIx input — they return Option (String × IxCursor input) (or the cursor-tuple variant). Their PSV reasoning therefore lives at the dispatcher level (scanNextTokenIx_dispatchContent, 6d.1e.6), where the dispatcher arm calls the primitive and then emitAts the resulting .scalar content style token. The plain-scalar case will need the scanPlainScalarIx_content_valid side condition from Proofs/Scanner/IndexedScalar.lean Layer F.4 (8 branch-mapping lemmas already in place) — staged in 6d.1e.6 either as a third axiom or proven inline depending on the Layer F.4 integration cost.

            §7a emitAt building blocks #

            emitAt-twins of the emit building blocks from §5. Both emit and emitAt push exactly one IxToken and differ only in the start position carried in the new token's .start field — irrelevant for PSV / FlowNestingInv / FlowContextPSV, which all dispatch on .token.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emitAt_tokens_size {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (startPos : YamlPos) (tok : YamlToken) (hOrder : startPos.offset ≤ s.cursor.pos.offset) :
            (s.emitAt startPos tok hOrder).tokens.size = s.tokens.size + 1

            Non-cursor record-update view of emitAt: tokens grow by one.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emitAt_preserves_tokens_at {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (startPos : YamlPos) (tok : YamlToken) (hOrder : startPos.offset ≤ s.cursor.pos.offset) (j : Nat) (h : j < s.tokens.size) :
            (s.emitAt startPos tok hOrder).tokens[j] = s.tokens[j]

            emitAt preserves tokens at low indices. emitAt-twin of emit_preserves_tokens_at.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emitAt_new_token_token {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (startPos : YamlPos) (tok : YamlToken) (hOrder : startPos.offset ≤ s.cursor.pos.offset) (h : s.tokens.size < (s.emitAt startPos tok hOrder).tokens.size) :
            (s.emitAt startPos tok hOrder).tokens[s.tokens.size].token = tok

            New-token characterization for emitAt. The token added at position s.tokens.size is exactly tok.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emitAt_non_plain_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (startPos : YamlPos) (tok : YamlToken) (hOrder : startPos.offset ≤ s.cursor.pos.offset) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) (h_np : match tok with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) :
            WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx (s.emitAt startPos tok hOrder).tokens

            emitAt of a non-plain token preserves PlainScalarsValidIx.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emitAt_non_flow_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (startPos : YamlPos) (tok : YamlToken) (hOrder : startPos.offset ≤ s.cursor.pos.offset) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) (h_nfs : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart) (h_nfe : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd) (h_nms : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart) (h_nme : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
            FlowNestingInvIx (s.emitAt startPos tok hOrder)

            emitAt of a non-flow token preserves FlowNestingInvIx. Mirrors emit_non_flow_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx, because flowNestingIx_push_non_flow only looks at the new token's .token (not its .start / .stop).

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emitAt_non_flow_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (startPos : YamlPos) (tok : YamlToken) (hOrder : startPos.offset ≤ s.cursor.pos.offset) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) (h_np : match tok with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) :
            WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx (s.emitAt startPos tok hOrder).tokens

            emitAt of a non-flow, non-plain token preserves FlowContextPSVIx.

            §7b scanAnchorOrAliasIx preservation — staged as axioms (Step 6d.1e.3) #

            scanAnchorOrAliasIx s isAnchor is .ok exactly when the anchor name is non-empty; on .ok, the new token is .anchor name (if isAnchor) or .alias name (otherwise). Neither is .scalar _ .plain nor a flow bracket.

            Staging note: these 8 axioms are pure scanner-side preservation lemmas — they only require the legacy proof patterns adapted through change/show bridging. Initial proof attempts hit the "record-update opacity" wall (the outer { sEmit with simpleKeyAllowed := false, definedAnchors := … } wrap doesn't let Array.getElem_push_eq fire via rw or simp without additional structural lemmas — see Reflection 70). Landed as axioms with real (_h_ok : scanAnchorOrAliasIx s isAnchor = .ok s') preconditions so downstream dispatchers (6d.1e.4+) can be built on top; discharge moves to a dedicated 6d.1e.3b session (or rolled into 6d.1e.7 alongside the §8 discharge).

            source
            📐 coverage

            s'.tokens.size = s.tokens.size + 1

            📐 coverage

            s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]

            s'.flowLevel = s.flowLevel

            match s'.tokens[s.tokens.size].token with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True

            s'.tokens[s.tokens.size].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ s'.tokens[s.tokens.size].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ s'.tokens[s.tokens.size].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ s'.tokens[s.tokens.size].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd

            WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens

            scanAnchorOrAliasIx preserves PlainScalarsValidIx — proven using the (staged-as-axiom) prefix + new-token-not-plain lemmas, so this composition theorem itself is a real theorem.

            §7c scanTagIx preservation — staged as axioms (Step 6d.1e.3) #

            Same staging rationale as §7b: scanTagIx s has three success branches all emitting .tag _ _ tokens; the proof shape is the legacy scanTag_psv_match adapted with change/show bridging, but the same record-update opacity wall (Reflection 70) prevents clean Lean 4 ports without additional structural lemmas. Discharged in a dedicated 6d.1e.3b or as part of 6d.1e.7.

            FlowNestingInvIx s'

            §8 Block-context dispatcher preservation (Step 6d.1e.4) #

            Preservation suites for the block-indicator scanners scanBlockEntryIx (-), scanKeyIx (?), scanValueIx (:), their sub-stages scanValueClearKeyIx / scanValuePrepareIx, and the umbrella dispatcher scanNextTokenIx_dispatchBlockIndicators.

            Strategy. The block-indicator scanners compose four kinds of state-transforming primitives:

            1. Pure record updates (scanValueClearKeyIx, simpleKeyAllowed tweaks, etc.) — tokens unchanged, preservation by rfl-style reasoning;
            2. pushSequenceIndentIx / pushMappingIndentIx (§6d/§6e) — emit a single non-plain non-flow indent-start token;
            3. s.emit YamlToken.{blockEntry,key,value} — emit a single non-plain non-flow indicator token (§5 building blocks);
            4. s.overwriteAtCursor i sk tok (only in scanValuePrepareIx) — setIfInBounds overwrite with .blockMappingStart or .key.

            §8a sets up the setIfInBounds infrastructure (PSV side; the FCPSV / FlowNestingInv side for setIfInBounds is staged as the single per-dispatcher axiom on scanValuePrepareIx because the non-flow-original requirement needs a token-stream invariant the indexed proof chain has not yet propagated — see §8e Reflection 71).

            §8b–§8d cover the per-scanner preservation suites; §8e wraps scanValuePrepareIx (PSV proven; FCPSV / FlowNestingInv staged as axioms); §8f composes the scanValueIx chain on top of §8a/§8e; §8g case-splits the umbrella scanNextTokenIx_dispatchBlockIndicators into its three .ok (some _) arms.

            §8a setIfInBounds PSV / FCPSV preservation primitives #

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.PlainScalarsValidIx_setIfInBounds_non_plain {input : String} (tokens : Indexed.TokenStream input) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx tokens) (idx : Nat) (t : Indexed.IxToken input) (h_np : match t.token with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) :
            WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx (tokens.setIfInBounds idx t)

            Pushing/overwriting via setIfInBounds with a non-plain element preserves PlainScalarsValidIx. The replaced slot becomes the new t; non-plain ⇒ the PSV match at that index is vacuously True. Indexed twin of legacy PlainScalarsValid_setIfInBounds_non_plain.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.overwriteAtCursor_tokens_size {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (i : Nat) (sk : Indexed.IxCursor input) (tok : YamlToken) :
            (s.overwriteAtCursor i sk tok).tokens.size = s.tokens.size

            overwriteAtCursor size invariance. The underlying setIfInBounds is size-preserving regardless of whether i is in bounds.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.overwriteAtCursor_non_plain_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (i : Nat) (sk : Indexed.IxCursor input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) (h_np : match tok with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) :
            WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx (s.overwriteAtCursor i sk tok).tokens

            overwriteAtCursor with a non-plain token preserves PlainScalarsValidIx.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.FlowContextPSVIx_setIfInBounds_non_flow {input : String} (tokens : Indexed.TokenStream input) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx tokens) (idx : Nat) (val : Indexed.IxToken input) (h_np : match val.token with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) (h_val_nf : val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ val.token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) (h_orig_nf : ∀ (h : idx < tokens.size), tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ tokens[idx].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
            WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx (tokens.setIfInBounds idx val)

            setIfInBounds with a non-plain, non-flow replacement preserves FlowContextPSVIx, provided the original token at the modified position is also non-flow. Indexed twin of legacy FlowContextPSV_setIfInBounds.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.overwriteAtCursor_non_plain_non_flow_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (i : Nat) (sk : Indexed.IxCursor input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) (h_np : match tok with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) (h_val_nf : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) (h_orig_nf : ∀ (h : i < s.tokens.size), s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
            WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx (s.overwriteAtCursor i sk tok).tokens

            overwriteAtCursor with a non-plain, non-flow token preserves FlowContextPSVIx, provided the original token at the modified position is non-flow.

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.overwriteAtCursor_non_flow_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (i : Nat) (sk : Indexed.IxCursor input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) (h_val_nf : tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ tok ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ tok ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) (h_orig_nf : ∀ (h : i < s.tokens.size), s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ s.tokens[i].token ≠ YamlToken.flowMappingEnd) :
            FlowNestingInvIx (s.overwriteAtCursor i sk tok)

            overwriteAtCursor with a non-flow token preserves FlowNestingInvIx, provided the original token at the modified position is non-flow.

            §8b scanValueClearKeyIx preservation #

            scanValueClearKeyIx is a pure state-only update on the simpleKey field — tokens are completely untouched. Every preservation lemma reduces to rfl after unfold; split.

            source
            📐 coverage
            @[simp]
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanValueClearKeyIx_tokens {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) :
            s.scanValueClearKeyIx.tokens = s.tokens

            scanValueClearKeyIx leaves the token stream unchanged.

            source
            📐 coverage
            @[simp]

            s.scanValueClearKeyIx.flowLevel = s.flowLevel

            scanValueClearKeyIx preserves flowLevel (no flow-level update).

            WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.scanValueClearKeyIx.tokens

            WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.scanValueClearKeyIx.tokens

            📐 coverage

            FlowNestingInvIx s.scanValueClearKeyIx

            §8c scanBlockEntryIx preservation #

            scanBlockEntryIx s = .ok s' iff either s.inFlow (no tab check fires) or !s.hasTabInPrecedingWhitespace. In both cases: s'.tokens = ((pushSequenceIndentIx-or-id s).emit .blockEntry).tokens (advance and the outer simpleKeyAllowed := true update do not touch tokens). Preservation composes §6d (pushSequenceIndentIx) with §5 (emit_non_plain / emit_non_flow).

            source
            📐 coverage

            WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
            source
            📐 coverage

            WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens
            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanBlockEntryIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanBlockEntryIx = Except.ok s') (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
            FlowNestingInvIx s'

            §8d scanKeyIx preservation #

            scanKeyIx s = .ok s' iff the inner tab-after-? check does not fire. In both context branches: s'.tokens = ((pushMappingIndentIx-or-id s).emit .key).tokens (the outer record-update on simpleKeyAllowed/explicitKeyLine/ simpleKey does not touch tokens, and advance is token-transparent). Composes §6e (pushMappingIndentIx) with §5 (emit_non_*).

            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanKeyIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanKeyIx = Except.ok s') (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
            WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanKeyIx_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanKeyIx = Except.ok s') (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) :
            WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens
            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanKeyIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanKeyIx = Except.ok s') (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
            FlowNestingInvIx s'

            §8e scanValuePrepareIx preservation — all three landed (6d.1e.10) #

            scanValuePrepareIx s either (a) overwrites token slots simpleKey.tokenIndex and simpleKey.tokenIndex + 1 with non-plain non-flow tokens (.blockMappingStart, .key), (b) leaves tokens unchanged (record-only updates), or (c) delegates to pushMappingIndentIx.

            PSV preservation is proven via §8a (setIfInBounds non-plain) + §6e (pushMappingIndentIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx).

            FCPSV / FlowNestingInv preservation also threads SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx (defined just below): the overwriteAtCursor branches need the original tokens at simpleKey.tokenIndex (and +1) to be .placeholder, which is non-flow, so setIfInBounds_non_flow discharges via §8a's overwriteAtCursor_non_plain_non_flow_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx and overwriteAtCursor_non_flow_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx. The invariant is established by saveSimpleKeyIx (§6f, two placeholder emits at simpleKey.tokenIndex/+1) and threaded through the dispatcher chain by the §11i↑ scanNextTokenIx_preserves_* plumbing landed in 6d.1e.10.

            source
            🔹 coverage
            def L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) :
            Prop

            SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s says: whenever s.simpleKey.possible is set, the token slots at s.simpleKey.tokenIndex and s.simpleKey.tokenIndex + 1 exist (are in-bounds) and hold .placeholder — the markers saveSimpleKeyIx reserved. Threaded through the dispatcher chain; consumed by scanValuePrepareIx to discharge the setIfInBounds_non_flow original-token obligation. Indexed twin of legacy SimpleKeyPlaceholderInv.

            Equations
            • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
            Instances For
              source
              ⬇️ coverage
              theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx_of_not_possible {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h : s.simpleKey.possible = false) :
              SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s

              Vacuous when possible = false.

              source
              📐 coverage
              theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.mk'_SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx (input : String) :
              SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx (Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx.mk' input)

              Initial state satisfies the invariant — mk' sets simpleKey.possible := false (its default).

              §6e+ AllKeysPlaceholderInvIx — full 4-tuple invariant #

              Indexed mirror of legacy AllKeysPlaceholderInv (lines 4264-4326 in Proofs/Production/ScannerPlainScalarValid.lean). The current-key SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx (defined above) is just the first conjunct; to thread the invariant through flow-start/flow-end scanners (which push the current key to a stack and later restore it) we additionally need:

              • SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx — every stacked key with possible = true still has .placeholder at its tokenIndex and +1.
              • SimpleKeyTokenDisjointIx — the current key's tokenIndex pair is strictly above every stacked key's pair, so setIfInBounds at the current key (in scanValuePrepareIx) cannot corrupt stacked placeholders.
              • SimpleKeyStackOrderingIx — stacked keys are themselves ordered by tokenIndex, so popping the top preserves disjointness for the new top.

              The combined AllKeysPlaceholderInvIx is what scanLoopIx_preserves_* threads; SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx is .1 of it and remains the direct dependency of scanValuePrepareIx_preserves_*.

              source
              🔹 coverage
              def L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) :
              Prop

              Every stacked key with possible = true is in bounds and its tokenIndex/+1 slots hold .placeholder. Indexed twin of legacy SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInv.

              Equations

                def L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.SimpleKeyTokenDisjointIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) :
                Prop

                Stacked key token-index pairs are strictly below the current key pair. Indexed twin of legacy SimpleKeyTokenDisjoint.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx_mono {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_phi : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) (h_sk : s'.simpleKey = s.simpleKey) (h_mono : s'.tokens.size ≥ s.tokens.size) (h_pref : ∀ (i : Nat) (h : i < s.tokens.size), s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]) :
                      SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s'

                      SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx mono: preserved when simpleKey is unchanged, tokens grow, and the existing prefix is preserved.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx_mono {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ssphi : SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx s) (h_stack : s'.simpleKeyStack = s.simpleKeyStack) (h_mono : s'.tokens.size ≥ s.tokens.size) (h_pref : ∀ (i : Nat) (h : i < s.tokens.size), s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]) :
                      SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx s'

                      SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx mono: preserved when stack is unchanged, tokens grow, prefix preserved.

                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx_of_empty {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h : s.simpleKeyStack.size = 0) :
                      SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx s

                      SimpleKeyStackPlaceholderInvIx vacuous when stack is empty.

                      source
                      ⬇️ coverage

                      SimpleKeyTokenDisjointIx s

                      Disjoint is vacuous when current possible = false.

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      SimpleKeyTokenDisjointIx s'

                      Disjoint preserved when simpleKey and stack are both unchanged.

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      SimpleKeyStackOrderingIx s'

                      Stack ordering preserved when stack is unchanged.

                      📐 coverage

                      AllKeysPlaceholderInvIx s'

                      ⬇️ coverage

                      source
                      ⬇️ coverage

                      AllKeysPlaceholderInvIx s'

                      Combined: cleared current + stack preserved via mono.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.mk'_AllKeysPlaceholderInvIx (input : String) :
                      AllKeysPlaceholderInvIx (Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx.mk' input)

                      Initial state satisfies AllKeysPlaceholderInvIx: current key has possible = false, stack is empty.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emit_preserves_SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_inv : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx (s.emit tok)

                      emit tok preserves SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx: it grows the token stream by one and leaves simpleKey unchanged, so the placeholders at simpleKey.tokenIndex/+1 remain in place.

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.scanValuePrepareIx.tokens

                      📐 coverage

                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.scanValuePrepareIx.tokens

                      scanValuePrepareIx preserves FlowContextPSVIx when the simple-key placeholder invariant holds. Discharged in 6d.1e.10 via the overwriteAtCursor_non_plain_non_flow_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx helper from §8a, with the original-token-non-flow obligation discharged by h_pl (the placeholders are non-flow).

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanValuePrepareIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) (h_pl : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s.scanValuePrepareIx

                      scanValuePrepareIx preserves FlowNestingInvIx when the simple-key placeholder invariant holds. Companion of scanValuePrepareIx_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx.

                      §8f scanValueIx preservation #

                      scanValueIx s = .ok s' iff scanValueValidateIx and scanValueTabCheckIx both succeed. On success: s'.tokens = ((scanValuePrepareIx (scanValueClearKeyIx s)).emit .value).advance.tokens (modulo the outer simpleKeyAllowed := true, explicitKeyLine := none record update). PSV / FCPSV / FNI all compose §8b (scanValueClearKeyIx)

                      • §8e (scanValuePrepareIx) + §5 (emit_non_*).
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanValueIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanValueIx = Except.ok s') (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanValueClearKeyIx_preserves_SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_inv : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s.scanValueClearKeyIx

                      scanValueClearKeyIx preserves SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx: it either leaves the state unchanged or clears simpleKey to its default (possible := false), so the invariant either holds transparently or is vacuously satisfied.

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      FlowNestingInvIx s'

                      §8g scanNextTokenIx_dispatchBlockIndicators preservation #

                      The umbrella dispatcher returns .ok (some s') iff exactly one of scanBlockEntryIx, scanKeyIx, scanValueIx succeeded. Case-split on the dispatch arm and apply §8c / §8d / §8f.

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchBlockIndicators_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchBlockIndicators c = Except.ok (some s')) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) (h_pl : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s'

                      §9 Top-level theorems — staged as axioms with tightened preconditions #

                      These are the two top-level theorems that the per-action preservation chain (Step 6d.1e.3+) will eventually establish. For now, they are declared as axioms with real Scanner.Indexed.scanIx input = .ok tokens preconditions (replacing the placeholder (h_from_scanner : True) hypotheses staged in IndexedWellBehaved.lean Step 6d.1c).

                      Phase 3 axiom budget: these two top-level axioms plus the scanner-side axioms staged in §7b/§7c (Step 6d.1e.3) and §8c/§8e (Step 6d.1e.4). All scanner-side axioms must be discharged before the §9 top-level axioms can be promoted to theorems; Step 6f cutover gates on the §9 promotion.

                      Consumers: parseStream_output_grammable (legacy: Proofs/Parser/ParserGrammable.lean:71-72; post-cutover: indexed analogue in the renamed ParserGrammable.lean) will obtain FlowAwarePSVIx and FlowBracketsMatchedIx for the parser-side chain by applying these.

                      §10 Flow-context dispatcher preservation (Step 6d.1e.5) #

                      Preservation suites for the flow-bracket scanners scanFlowSequenceStartIx ([), scanFlowSequenceEndIx (]), scanFlowMappingStartIx ({), scanFlowMappingEndIx (}), scanFlowEntryIx (,), and the umbrella dispatcher scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators.

                      Strategy. Unlike the block-context dispatchers in §8, the flow scanners emit flow tokens themselves. PSV preservation still follows the standard "emit non-plain" recipe (every flow bracket is non-plain). FCPSV preservation needs a slight relaxation of the §5 building block: emit_non_flow_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx forbids the new token from being a flow bracket, but the FCPSV proof body never actually uses that hypothesis — the new-token discharge goes through fpsv_of_not_plain_ix, which only cares about non-plain. §10a adds the cleaner emit_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx variant.

                      FNI preservation is the genuinely new piece: the scanner's flowLevel shifts by ±1 (open / close), and flowNestingIx on the token-array side shifts in lockstep via flowNestingIx_push (§2). For .flowSequenceEnd / .flowMappingEnd, the underflow case (s.flowLevel = 0) is handled uniformly by Nat monus (0 - 1 = 0) — the dispatcher's runtime check prevents this case in practice, but the FNI lemma holds unconditionally.

                      §10a sets up the FCPSV emit building block; §10b–§10e cover the four bracket scanners (each 3 lemmas: PSV / FCPSV / FNI); §10f wraps scanFlowEntryIx on top of §8e (depends on the two §8e axioms for FCPSV / FNI but produces a real theorem statement); §10g case-splits the dispatcher into its five .ok (some _) arms.

                      §10a Generic emit-step preservation for flow brackets #

                      emit_non_flow_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx requires the emitted token to not be a flow bracket — useful for block-context scanners, useless for flow-bracket scanners. The proof body of that lemma never consumes the non-flow hypotheses (they sit in underscored arguments), so the FCPSV preservation is really a non-plain-only fact. The variant below records that.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.emit_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (tok : YamlToken) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) (h_np : match tok with | YamlToken.scalar value ScalarStyle.plain => False | x => True) :
                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx (s.emit tok).tokens

                      Emitting a non-plain token preserves FlowContextPSVIx. Companion to emit_non_flow_non_plain_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx (§5) — drops the four non-flow hypotheses, which the proof body does not consume. Used by the flow-bracket scanner preservation suites in §10b–§10e.

                      §10b scanFlowSequenceStartIx preservation #

                      scanFlowSequenceStartIx s = { (s.emit .flowSequenceStart).advance with flowLevel := _ + 1, flowStack := _, simpleKeyStack := _, simpleKey := _, simpleKeyAllowed := true }. The record-update touches flowLevel (the FNI-relevant field) and several fields invisible to our predicates; .tokens is unchanged by the record update, so s'.tokens = (s.emit .flowSequenceStart).tokens (after advance_tokens).

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowSequenceStartIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.scanFlowSequenceStartIx.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowSequenceStartIx_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.scanFlowSequenceStartIx.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowSequenceStartIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s.scanFlowSequenceStartIx

                      §10c scanFlowSequenceEndIx preservation #

                      Symmetric to §10b but for ]: emits .flowSequenceEnd, advances, and sets flowLevel := _ - 1. Note: scanFlowSequenceEndIx itself does not check s.flowLevel > 0 — that's the dispatcher's job in §10g. The FNI lemma holds unconditionally because Nat monus saturates at zero (0 - 1 = 0) and flowNestingIx_push mirrors that exactly.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowSequenceEndIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.scanFlowSequenceEndIx.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowSequenceEndIx_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.scanFlowSequenceEndIx.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowSequenceEndIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s.scanFlowSequenceEndIx

                      §10d scanFlowMappingStartIx preservation #

                      Same shape as §10b with .flowMappingStart in place of .flowSequenceStart. The flowNestingIx_push match treats them identically (both depth + 1).

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      §10e scanFlowMappingEndIx preservation #

                      Same shape as §10c with .flowMappingEnd in place of .flowSequenceEnd.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowMappingEndIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.scanFlowMappingEndIx.tokens

                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowMappingEndIx_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.scanFlowMappingEndIx.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowMappingEndIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s.scanFlowMappingEndIx

                      §10f scanFlowEntryIx preservation #

                      After Step 6f.0, scanFlowEntryIx s is a do-block: it first checks lastRealTokenValIx? s.tokens and throws invalidFlowEntry on a leading or consecutive ,; otherwise it succeeds with s' = { (s.emit .flowEntry).advance with simpleKeyAllowed := true }. The earlier accidental scanValuePrepareIx s call was removed (the , boundary does not retroactively confirm the pending simple key).

                      .flowEntry is non-plain and non-flow-bracket, so the §5 emit_non_* building blocks apply directly to s itself — no scanValuePrepareIx composition needed.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowEntryIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanFlowEntryIx = Except.ok s') (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowEntryIx_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanFlowEntryIx = Except.ok s') (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) (_h_pl : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanFlowEntryIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanFlowEntryIx = Except.ok s') (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) (_h_pl : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s'

                      §10g scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators preservation #

                      The umbrella dispatcher case-splits on c ∈ { '[', ']', '{', '}', ',' }, each producing .ok (some s') via the corresponding §10b–§10f lemma (with a flowLevel == 0 runtime guard on ] / } / ,). The none fall-through case (none of the five characters) returns .ok none and is therefore inconsistent with the .ok (some s') hypothesis.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators c = Except.ok (some s')) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators c = Except.ok (some s')) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) (h_pl : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanNextTokenIx_dispatchFlowIndicators c = Except.ok (some s')) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) (h_pl : SimpleKeyPlaceholderInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s'

                      §11 Document/directive + top-level dispatch composition (Step 6d.1e.6) #

                      Preservation suites for the document/directive layer plus the scanNextTokenIx top-level dispatcher and the scanLoopIx recursive loop.

                      Staging strategy (Reflection 73, new this session). The full preservation chain for the document/directive + content/preprocess layers — and the scanNextTokenIx top-level composition itself — all hit one of three structural walls:

                      1. Reflection 70 (record-update opacity): the leaf scanners end with multi-field record updates over the post-emit state, blocking rfl/simp reductions through .tokens / .flowLevel accessors.

                      2. let-binding wall: dispatchers chain multiple let bindings around inner if/match that split at h_ok cannot peel through without an interposing dsimp only [], and even with dsimp the pair-destructure of preprocess's .ok (some (s2, c)) output is ambiguous in Lean 4 (Lean greedily destructures ScannerStateIx's 15 fields when given an anonymous pair-pattern).

                      3. Layer F.4 dependency: scanNextTokenIx_dispatchContent's plain-scalar arm needs ScalarScannable from Proofs/Scanner/IndexedScalar.lean (Reflection 72).

                      All three walls fall to the same 6d.1e.7 discharge effort, so every leaf, intermediate dispatcher, and the top-level scanNextTokenIx in §11a–§11i is staged as axioms with real .ok preconditions, leaving §11j (scanLoopIx_preserves_*) — the layer that finally produces the three closure invariants from the fuel-recursion — as real theorems. The composition shape is: 27 staged axioms + 3 real-theorem scanLoopIx_preserves_* lemmas on top.

                      Axiom budget update: 6d.1e.6 lands 27 new axioms on top of the 16 staged in 6d.1e.3/6d.1e.4/6d.1e.5. Total: 43 staged axioms to discharge in 6d.1e.7. The budget revision is justified because all 27 of the new axioms fall to the same set of resolution techniques (record-update opacity peeling, let-binding dsimp chain unfolding, Layer F.4 ScalarScannable integration) — once those substrate fixes land in 6d.1e.7, the 27 axioms discharge in a single sweeping session.

                      §11a–§11h: 24 staged axioms (4 leaf scanners × 3 invariants + 4 dispatchers × 3 invariants). §11i: 3 staged axioms (scanNextTokenIx). §11j: 3 real theorems (scanLoopIx_preserves_*).

                      §11a scanDocumentStartIx preservation — proven #

                      Per the Wall #1 probe (Reflection 70 discharge): the outer record update on simpleKeyAllowed, allowDirectives, etc. is a pure record update on non-tokens/non-flowLevel fields, so it's defeq for both .tokens and .flowLevel. Combined with advanceN-preserves-tokens-and-flowLevel (defeq) and the { s with simpleKey := ... }-preserves-tokens-and-flowLevel (defeq), the function reduces to ((unwindIndentsIx s (-1)).emit .documentStart) for both projections. PSV/FCPSV use the §5 emit_*_preserves_* chain; FNI additionally uses unwindIndentsIx_preserves_flowLevel as a rewrite (not a defeq).

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanDocumentStartIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.scanDocumentStartIx.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanDocumentStartIx_preserves_FlowContextPSVIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_old : WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s.scanDocumentStartIx.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanDocumentStartIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s.scanDocumentStartIx

                      §11b scanDocumentEndIx preservation — proven #

                      Mirrors the legacy scanDocumentEnd_preserves_FlowNestingInv pattern: factor out the base preservation h_base for the post-emit .documentEnd state, then case-split on the trailing-content match (probe.peek? + isLineBreakBool); every non-throwing arm produces the same s' so all reduce to h_base.

                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanDocumentEndIx_preserves_PlainScalarsValidIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_de : s.scanDocumentEndIx = Except.ok s') (h_old : WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s.tokens) :
                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanDocumentEndIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_de : s.scanDocumentEndIx = Except.ok s') (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s'

                      §11c scanYamlDirectiveIx preservation — proven #

                      scanYamlDirectiveIx either throws or produces an .ok state via emitAt startPos (.versionDirective major minor) hBound, then wraps with { ... with seenYamlDirective := true, directivesPresent := true }. For tokens/flowLevel, both the outer record update and the inner sAfter = { s with cursor := ... } are defeq-transparent. The proof mirrors §11b's structure: case-split on the two ifs, base preservation via §7a emitAt_*_preserves_* applied at sAfter.

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens
                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens

                      📐 coverage
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.Indexed.ScannerPlainScalarValid.scanYamlDirectiveIx_preserves_FlowNestingInvIx {input : String} (s : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (cAfterWS : Indexed.IxCursor input) (startPos : YamlPos) (hStart : startPos.offset ≤ cAfterWS.pos.offset) (s' : Scanner.Indexed.ScannerStateIx input) (h_ok : s.scanYamlDirectiveIx cAfterWS startPos hStart = Except.ok s') (h_fni : FlowNestingInvIx s) :
                      FlowNestingInvIx s'

                      §11d scanTagDirectiveIx preservation — proven #

                      Same structure as §11c but without the second if (the tag directive emits unconditionally on the .ok path, since collectTagHandleLoopIx and collectTagSuffixLoopIx always return — there's no empty-string fail mode like the YAML major/minor parser).

                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens

                      §11e scanDirectiveIx preservation — proven #

                      Composition over §11c (YAML branch) / §11d (TAG branch) / identity (default branch). The let-binding chain (Reflection 73 wall) is peeled cleanly because split at h_ok operates on the inner if name == "YAML" then ... else if name == "TAG" then ... else .ok ... without needing to evaluate the intermediate lets — the goal/hypothesis stays well-formed through each arm.

                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens

                      WellBehaved.FlowContextPSVIx s'.tokens

                      FlowNestingInvIx s'

                      §11f scanNextTokenIx_dispatchStructural preservation — proven #

                      Three success arms: scanDocumentStartIx (§11a), scanDocumentEndIx (§11b), scanDirectiveIx (§11e). Each if early-throws or short-circuits; the legacy repeat (any_goals (split at h_ok)) peeling pattern is replaced here by explicit nested split at h_ok since each leaf composes into a different sub-proof.

                      WellBehaved.PlainScalarsValidIx s'.tokens

                      §11g scanNextTokenIx_preprocess preservation — staged as axioms #

                      Reflection 74 wall (new): even after unfold/rw, the have-encoded inner let-bindings (have savedIndentSize := ...; have s := ...) block split at h_ok from peeling deeper into the body's nested if chain, and dsimp only [] reports "no progress" because Lean does not zeta-reduce letFun/have-encoded bindings by default in hypothesis position. Staged for substrate-fix in a follow-up step (extract_lets at h_ok or equivalent). The dispatcher chain (§11i) takes these as axiomatic inputs.

                      source
                      📐 coverage

                      FlowNestingInvIx s.skipToContentS

                      skipToContentS preserves FlowNestingInvIx: tokens and flowLevel are unchanged (the function only updates the cursor).
